Off Calendar May-20-2011 Continued To Jul-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MILENA TALIAN, MISSION BAY HOUSING PARTNERS, LP, FILLMORE MARKETPLACE, LP, AND RELATED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LP'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Jacqueline Johnson, 409-2704 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Alison Yew, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP 362-2650 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Discussed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -
Defendants' Demurrer to the 1st cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to succinctly state that a duty was owed and how that duty was breached by these defendants.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to succinctly state that a duty was owed and how that duty was breached by these defendants.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend, Plaintiff needs to attach a copy of the lease agreement to the First Amended Complaint.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 4th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. There is no such thing as "City Ordinance 2002."
Defendants' Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. This is a federal claim that cannot be pled in this Court.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 6th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to clarify what the basis for this cause of action is. Specifically, plaintiff needs to allege what these defendants actually did to cause the problems alleged and how the problems were forms of discrimination against the plaintiff.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 7th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to plead with more specificity the fact upon which she bases this claim.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 8th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to allege what type of discrimination she suffered from based upon the alleged acts by the defendants.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 9th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. None of the stated fact are sufficient to state a cause of action against the defendants.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 10th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to plead facts that show extreme and outrageous conduct by defendant that caused her to suffer severe emotional distress.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 11th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Because plaintiff's cause of action for Breach of Contract fails for the reasons given above, this cause of action fails as well. In addition, the Court indicated that the amended complaint is to be filed by 05/27/11. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
